530		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "d", function()
531			lain.util.delete_tag()
532		end, { description = "delete tag", group = "tag" }),
533	
534		-- Volume control
535		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "=", function()
536			awful.spawn("amixer set Master 5%+")
537			-- Update volume widget if it exists
538			if theme.volume and theme.volume.update then
539				theme.volume.update()
540			end
541		end, { description = "volume up", group = "audio" }),
542		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "-", function()
543			awful.spawn("amixer set Master 5%-")
544			-- Update volume widget if it exists
545			if theme.volume and theme.volume.update then
546				theme.volume.update()
547			end
548		end, { description = "volume down", group = "audio" }),
549

551		awful.key({ MODKEY, altkey }, "Right", function()
552			awful.spawn("playerctl next")
553		end, { description = "next track", group = "audio" }),
554		awful.key({ MODKEY, altkey }, "Left", function()
555			awful.spawn("playerctl previous")
556		end, { description = "previous track", group = "audio" }),
557		awful.key({ MODKEY, altkey }, "space", function()
558			awful.spawn("playerctl play-pause")
559		end, { description = "play/pause", group = "audio" })
560	)

562	CLIENTKEYS = gears.table.join(
563		awful.key({ altkey, "Shift" }, "m", lain.util.magnify_client, { description = "magnify client", group = "client" }),
564		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "f", function(c)
565			c.fullscreen = not c.fullscreen
566			c:raise()
567		end, { description = "toggle fullscreen", group = "client" }),
568		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "c", function(c)
569			c:kill()
570		end, { description = "close", group = "client" }),
571		awful.key(
572			{ MODKEY, "Control" },
573			"space",
574			awful.client.floating.toggle,
575			{ description = "toggle floating", group = "client" }
576		),
577		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Control" }, "Return", function(c)
578			c:swap(awful.client.getmaster())
579		end, { description = "move to master", group = "client" }),
580		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "o", function(c)
581			c:move_to_screen()
582		end, { description = "move to screen", group = "client" }),
583		awful.key({ MODKEY, "Shift" }, "y", function(c)
584			c.ontop = not c.ontop
585		end, { description = "toggle keep on top", group = "client" }),
586		awful.key({ MODKEY }, "y", function(c)
587			c.sticky = not c.sticky
588		end, { description = "toggle sticky", group = "client" }),

590			-- The client currently has the input focus, so it cannot be

1	-------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Drop-down applications manager for the awesome window manager
3	-------------------------------------------------------------------
4	-- Coded  by: * Lucas de Vries <lucas@glacicle.com>
5	-- Hacked by: * Adrian C. (anrxc) <anrxc@sysphere.org>
6	-- Licensed under the WTFPL version 2
7	--   * http://sam.zoy.org/wtfpl/COPYING
8	-------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- To use this module add:
10	--   local scratch = require("scratch")
11	-- to the top of your rc.lua, and call it from a keybinding:
12	--   scratch.drop(prog, vert, horiz, width, height, sticky, screen)
13	--
14	-- Parameters:
15	--   prog   - Program to run; "urxvt", "gmrun", "thunderbird"
16	--   vert   - Vertical; "bottom", "center" or "top" (default)
17	--   horiz  - Horizontal; "left", "right" or "center" (default)
18	--   width  - Width in absolute pixels, or width percentage
19	--            when <= 1 (1 (100% of the screen) by default)
20	--   height - Height in absolute pixels, or height percentage
21	--            when <= 1 (0.25 (25% of the screen) by default)
22	--   sticky - Visible on all tags, false by default
23	--   screen - Screen (optional), mouse.screen by default
24	-------------------------------------------------------------------
25	
26	-- Grab environment
27	local pairs = pairs
28	local awful = require("awful")
29	local setmetatable = setmetatable
30	local capi = {
31		mouse = mouse,
32		client = client,
33		screen = screen,
34	}
35	
36	-- Scratchdrop: drop-down applications manager for the awesome window manager
37	local drop = {} -- module scratch.drop
38	
39	local dropdown = {}
40	
41	-- Create a new window for the drop-down application when it doesn't
42	-- exist, or toggle between hidden and visible states when it does
43	function toggle(prog, vert, horiz, width, height, sticky, screen)
44		vert = vert or "top"
45		horiz = horiz or "center"
46		width = width or 1
47		height = height or 0.25
48		sticky = sticky or false
49		screen = screen or capi.mouse.screen
50	
51		-- Determine signal usage in this version of awesome
52		local attach_signal = capi.client.connect_signal or capi.client.add_signal
53		local detach_signal = capi.client.disconnect_signal or capi.client.remove_signal
54	
55		if not dropdown[prog] then
56			dropdown[prog] = {}
57	
58			-- Add unmanage signal for scratchdrop programs
59			attach_signal("unmanage", function(c)
60				for scr, cl in pairs(dropdown[prog]) do
61					if cl == c then
62						dropdown[prog][scr] = nil
63					end
64				end
65			end)
66		end
67	
68		if not dropdown[prog][screen] then
69			spawnw = function(c)
70				dropdown[prog][screen] = c
71	
72				-- Scratchdrop clients are floaters
73				awful.client.floating.set(c, true)
74	
75				-- Client geometry and placement
76				local screengeom = capi.screen[screen].workarea
77	
78				if width <= 1 then
79					width = screengeom.width * width
80				end
81				if height <= 1 then
82					height = screengeom.height * height
83				end
84	
85				if horiz == "left" then
86					x = screengeom.x
87				elseif horiz == "right" then
88					x = screengeom.width - width
89				else
90					x = screengeom.x + (screengeom.width - width) / 2
91				end
92	
93				if vert == "bottom" then
94					y = screengeom.height + screengeom.y - height
95				elseif vert == "center" then
96					y = screengeom.y + (screengeom.height - height) / 2
97				else
98					y = screengeom.y - screengeom.y
99				end
100	
101				-- Client properties
102				c:geometry({ x = x, y = y, width = width, height = height })
103				c.ontop = true
104				c.above = true
105				c.skip_taskbar = true
106				if sticky then

Let me provide you with a comprehensive summary of the floating popup window patterns used in this config. ## Floating Popup Window Patterns in Your Awesome Config I found **excellent examples** of floating popup implementation throughout your config. Here are the actual code patterns you need: ### 1. **`awful.popup` - Main Pattern (Recommended)** All your widget popups use `awful.popup`, which is the modern Awesome way to create floating windows. Here's the core structure from your widgets: **Basic Structure** (from `notif_widget.lua`, `todo_widget.lua`, `rss_widget.lua`): ```lua popup = awful.popup { widget = { rows, -- Your content (wibox.layout or widget) bg = bg_popup, widget = wibox.container.background, }, placement = function(w) awful.placement.top_right(w, { honor_workarea = true, margins = { top = 18, right = 0 }, }) end, shape = gears.shape.rounded_rect, border_width = 2, border_color = fg_green, ontop = true, visible = true, minimum_width = 200, maximum_width = 400, } ``` **Key Properties:** - `widget`: The content to display - `placement`: Function to position the popup (top_right, center_vertical, etc.) - `ontop`: Keep above other windows - `visible`: Show/hide toggle - `shape`, `border_width`, `border_color`: Styling - `minimum_width`, `maximum_width`: Constraints ### 2. **Show/Hide Pattern (Toggle Behavior)** All your widgets use this pattern: ```lua local popup = nil local function close_popup() if popup then popup.visible = false popup = nil end end local function show_popup() close_popup() -- Close any existing popup first -- Build your content local rows = wibox.layout.fixed.vertical() -- ... add rows to layout ... -- Create popup popup = awful.popup { widget = rows, -- ... other properties ... visible = true, } -- Auto-close on mouse leave or button press popup:connect_signal("mouse::leave", function() close_popup() end) popup:connect_signal("button::press", function() close_popup() end) end -- Widget button binding widget:buttons(gears.table.join( awful.button({}, 1, function() if popup then close_popup() else show_popup() end end) )) ``` ### 3. **Calendar Popup - Hover Pattern** (from `clock_widget.lua`) For popup on hover (without clicking): ```lua local cal_popup = require("awful.widget.calendar_popup").month local cal_popup = calendar_popup({ start_sunday = false, spacing = 10, style_month = { border_width = 2, border_color = color, padding = 8, bg_color = bg, fg_color = fg, shape = gears.shape.rounded_rect, }, -- ... other styles ... }) -- Attach on hover widget:connect_signal("mouse::enter", function() cal_popup:attach(widget, "tr", { on_hover = true }) end) widget:connect_signal("mouse::leave", function() cal_popup.visible = false end) ``` ### 4. **Confirmation Popup Pattern** (from `notif_widget.lua` and `rss_widget.lua`) For nested popups with buttons: ```lua local yes_btn = wibox.container.background( wibox.container.margin( wibox.widget { markup = 'Yes', widget = wibox.widget.textbox, }, 8, 8, 3, 3 ), "#374247", gears.shape.rounded_rect ) yes_btn:connect_signal("button::press", function() confirm_popup.visible = false -- do action end) yes_btn:connect_signal("mouse::enter", function() yes_btn.bg = "#4a5e53" -- Highlight on hover end) confirm_popup = awful.popup { widget = wibox.container.margin( wibox.widget { yes_btn, no_btn, layout = wibox.layout.fixed.horizontal, }, 10, 10, 8, 8 ), -- ... properties ... visible = true, } ``` ### 5. **Dropdown Window Pattern** (from `scratch/drop.lua`) For persistent floating windows that toggle visibility: ```lua -- Uses client properties, not awful.popup awful.client.floating.set(c, true) c:geometry({ x = x, y = y, width = width, height = height }) c.ontop = true c.above = true c.skip_taskbar = true -- Toggle visibility if c:isvisible() == false then c.hidden = true awful.client.movetotag(awful.tag.selected(screen), c) else if c.hidden then c.hidden = false c:raise() else c.hidden = true -- detach from tags end end ``` ### 6. **Key Differences: `awful.popup` vs `wibar` vs `client.floating`** | Method | Use Case | Persistence | |--------|----------|-------------| | **`awful.popup`** | Temporary popups (menus, info) | Created/destroyed on show/hide | | **`awful.wibar`** | Always-visible bars (taskbar) | Persistent during session | | **`client.floating`** | Full windows with decorations | Window manager client | | **`scratch.drop`** | Dropdown terminals/apps | Persistent, toggles visibility | ### 7. **Notification History Widget** (`notif_widget.lua`) This is the main example you mentioned - it intercepts notifications and displays them: **File:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/themes/everforest/widgets_config/notif_widget.lua` Key pattern: ```lua -- Intercept naughty.notify local _orig_notify = naughty.notify naughty.notify = function(args) local title = (args and args.title) or "" local message = (args and args.text) or "" table.insert(history, { title = title, message = message, time = os.date("%H:%M") }) return _orig_notify(args) -- Still send the notification end -- Then create popup from history on click ``` ### Summary Files 1. **Notification History Widget:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/themes/everforest/widgets_config/notif_widget.lua` (125-147 for popup creation) 2. **Todo Widget with Popups:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/themes/everforest/widgets_config/todo_widget.lua` (206-228 for popup) 3. **RSS Widget with Scrollable Popup:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/themes/everforest/widgets_config/rss_widget.lua` (302-337 for popup) 4. **Clock with Calendar Popup:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/themes/everforest/widgets_config/clock_widget.lua` (42-47 for hover popup) 5. **Dropdown Apps:** `/home/tymoyato/.dotfiles/dotfiles/awesome/scratch/drop.lua` (43-152 for client-based floating) The most flexible and modern approach is **`awful.popup`** with the show/hide toggle pattern - it's what all your widgets use!
